teaching.concept: A checklist uses bullets for tasks and inline code for the exact command to type.
teaching.howTo: Type `#` and `##` for the headings, `- ` before each task, and wrap the command in backticks.
teaching.example:
# Shutdown note

Three things at closing time.

## Before you go

- Count the drawer
- Run `report end` on the tablet
- Cover the stand

candidates.title: Short checklist
candidates.prompt: Rebuild the short checklist: one title, one H2 section, three bullets, and keep the command as inline code.
candidates.target:
# Kiosk shutdown checklist

Do these when the market closes.

## Before you go

- Count the cash drawer
- Run `report daily` on the tablet
- Cover the display shelf

matchChecks.feedback: Keep the note within 16 lines, like the Goal.
Match the Goal's block order exactly.
Use exactly 1 H2 section heading.
Keep one H1 title followed by H2 sections.
Use a bullet list with 3 visible items in this section.
Wrap the command in single backticks so it reads as code.

editorialChecks.review: This form reads best without italics; save emphasis for the bold deadline style.

hints: Every task line starts with `- ` and one space.
Wrap only the command in single backticks.
Pattern: `- Run \`command\` on the machine`.

teaching.concept: A decision note quotes the rule with a blockquote and numbers the steps in order.
teaching.howTo: Type `#` and `##` for the headings, `> ` before the decision, and `1. 2. 3.` before the steps.
teaching.example:
# Print note

Where big jobs go now.

## Decision

> Big jobs use the big printer.

## Steps

1. Check the size
2. Choose the printer

candidates.title: Team decision note
candidates.prompt: Rebuild the short decision note: one title, a blockquote under Decision, and three numbered steps.
candidates.target:
# Printer queue change

Large jobs move to the copy room printer.

## Decision

> Send jobs over ten pages to the copy room.

## Steps

1. Count your pages
2. Pick the right printer
3. Collect the printout

matchChecks.feedback: Keep the note within 16 lines, like the Goal.
Match the Goal's block order exactly.
Use exactly 2 H2 section headings.
Keep one H1 title followed by H2 sections.
Write the decision as one blockquote in the Decision section.
Use a numbered list with 3 visible items in this section.

editorialChecks.review: This form reads best without italics; save emphasis for the bold deadline style.

hints: The decision line starts with `> ` and one space.
The steps use `1. `, `2. `, `3. ` with one space after the dot.
Pattern: `# Title`, `## Decision`, `> Rule`, `## Steps`, `1. Step`.

teaching.concept: A decision note quotes the rule with a blockquote and numbers the steps in order.

teaching.concept: A handoff note uses one H1 title and H2 sections so the next person can scan it.
teaching.howTo: Type `#` before the title, `##` before each section name, and `- ` before each item.
teaching.example:
# Shift notes

What the next crew should know.

## Today

- Delivery stored
- Machine cleaned

candidates.title: Shift handoff note
candidates.prompt: Rebuild the short handoff note: one title, two H2 sections, and a two-item bullet list in each section.

- Milk delivery arrived
- Espresso machine cleaned

## Tomorrow

- Post the new menu card

teaching.concept: A status note bolds the one deadline and splits work into Done bullets and Next numbered steps.
teaching.howTo: Type `#` and `##` for the headings, `**` around the deadline, `- ` for Done, and `1. 2.` for Next.
teaching.example:
# Move status

Short update. **One bold cutoff date.**

## Done

- List copied
- Note drafted

## Next

1. Send a test
2. Close the old one

candidates.title: Status update note
candidates.prompt: Rebuild the short status note: one bold sentence, a two-item bullet list, and two numbered steps.
candidates.target:
# Newsletter move status

The new sender is ready. **The old address stops Monday.**

## Done

- Reader list copied
- Welcome note drafted

## Next

1. Send the test issue
2. Retire the old address

matchChecks.feedback: Keep the note within 16 lines, like the Goal.
Match the Goal's block order exactly.
Use exactly 2 H2 section headings.
Keep one H1 title followed by H2 sections.
Make the deadline sentence bold with double asterisks.
Use a bullet list with 2 visible items in this section.
Use a numbered list with 2 visible items in this section.

editorialChecks.review: This form reads best without italics; save emphasis for the bold deadline style.

hints: Wrap the deadline sentence in double asterisks: `**like this**`.
Done uses `- ` bullets; Next uses `1. ` and `2. `.
Pattern: `# Title`, bold sentence, `## Done`, `## Next`.
